messages.content: Can you write this differently
Could you please provide an alternative prompt or specific sentence for me to rewrite?
Can you write this differently,  •	An initial briefing about the project by the client, with confirmation of agreement to contract terms and any questions.

Sure, here's an alternative way to phrase it:

• The client initially provides a project briefing, while confirming agreement to contract terms and addressing any questions.

Can you write this differently, •	Completion of probity forms such as a Deed of Confidentiality, a Declaration of Conflict of Interest and written confirmation of your agreement to abide by the communication protocols set out in the client’s project Probity Plan

Certainly! Here's an alternative wording:

• The completion of necessary probity forms, including a Deed of Confidentiality, a Declaration of Conflict of Interest, and a written confirmation of your commitment to adhere to the communication protocols outlined in the client's project Probity Plan.
Can you write this differently, •	Handover of available project data by the client, including project objectives and project concep

Certainly! Here's an alternative phrasing:

• The transfer of project data from the client, which encompasses the provision of available information, project objectives, and project concept.
Can you write this better, Due diligence study of the site/building to identify any issues that may increase the risk and or cost of the project

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Conducting a thorough due diligence study of the site or building to carefully identify potential issues that could escalate both project risks and costs.

How would you write this better, o	Title search to check ownership of the site and any encumbrances such as easements, or restrictions, for example: multiple ownership, requirements under a 173 Agreement with the Municipal Council, caveats

To enhance the phrasing, here's an improved version:

• Conducting a comprehensive title search to verify site ownership and identify any encumbrances, such as easements, restrictions, multiple ownerships, obligations outlined in a 173 Agreement with the Municipal Council, or caveats.
Can you write this differently, o	Cadastral survey to show boundaries of the lot or lots, and to plot the title search information, including surrounding properties

Certainly! Here's an alternative phrasing:

• Undertaking a cadastral survey to accurately map the boundaries of the lot or lots, and to visually represent the information gathered from the title search, including nearby properties.

Can you rephrase this better, o	Topographical survey including contours, site orientation and dimensions, site features for example: underground and overhead service locations, existing trees, existing buildings, car parking spaces

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Conducting a comprehensive topographical survey that encompasses key aspects such as contour mapping, site orientation, dimensions, and notable features including the locations of underground and overhead services, existing trees, buildings, and available car parking spaces.
Can you write this better, o	Geotechnical investigation to check soil type and bearing pressure of underlying geomorphology and water table;

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Conducting a thorough geotechnical investigation to assess the soil type, bearing pressure of the underlying geomorphology, and water table conditions.

Can you write this better, o	Drainage and flood assessment to check flooding, for example: 1 in 100-year flood incidence level (or the chance of a flood reaching this level every 100 years: in this case 1% incidence);

Certainly! Here's a rephrased version:

• Conducting a comprehensive drainage and flood assessment to evaluate flood risks, including factors such as the 1 in 100-year flood incidence level, which represents a 1% chance of a flood reaching or exceeding this level within a 100-year period.
Can you write this better, o	Land use requirements such as land zoning with limitations on sub-division, density, uses, height, set-backs, car parking and other rules

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Assessing land use requirements, including land zoning regulations that impose limitations on sub-division, density, permitted uses, building height, set-backs, car parking provisions, and other relevant rules and regulations.
Can you write this better, o	Biodiversity study to check on the presence of rare and endangered species of flora and fauna and protection requirements;

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Conducting a comprehensive biodiversity study to assess the presence of rare and endangered species of flora and fauna, as well as determining the necessary protection requirements for their conservation.
Can you write this better, o	Net gain requirements for vegetation removal as required by Municipal Councils

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Evaluating the net gain requirements imposed by Municipal Councils for vegetation removal, ensuring compliance with regulations aimed at achieving a positive balance in vegetation preservation and restoration efforts.
Can you write this better, o	Indigenous and non-indigenous heritage requirements, for example: protection of archaeological sites, buildings, trees, views, monuments

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Addressing both indigenous and non-indigenous heritage requirements, such as the preservation of archaeological sites, buildings, trees, views, and monuments, to ensure the protection and conservation of cultural and historical heritage.
Can you write this better, o	Environmental investigation to check for possible soil and water contamination, buried waste, fuel or septic tanks

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Conducting a comprehensive environmental investigation to assess potential soil and water contamination, buried waste, and the presence of fuel or septic tanks, ensuring proper evaluation and mitigation of any environmental risks.

Can you write this differently, o	Municipal council assets – their existence and condition, for example: stormwater drains, drain covers, roads, kerbs and gutters, footpaths and crossovers, fences nature strips, signs, bollards, bins and seats

Certainly! Here's an alternative phrasing:

• Evaluating the existence and condition of municipal council assets, including but not limited to stormwater drains, drain covers, roads, kerbs and gutters, footpaths and crossovers, fences, nature strips, signs, bollards, bins, and seating facilities.
Can you rephrase this, o	Utility provider above ground assets – for example gas meters, fire hydrants, electricity sub-stations, poles, wires and streetlights, traffic lights and controllers

Certainly! Here's a rephrase:

• Assessing above-ground assets belonging to utility providers, including gas meters, fire hydrants, electricity sub-stations, poles, wires, streetlights, traffic lights, and controllers.
Can you write this better, o	Traffic management requirements for the site and surrounds including car parking requirements;

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Addressing traffic management requirements for the site and surrounding areas, including necessary considerations for car parking arrangements.
Can you rewrite this, o	Supply Authority services for water, sewer, gas, electricity, communications;

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Assessing the provision of essential services by Supply Authorities, including water, sewer, gas, electricity, and communication infrastructure.
Can you rewrite this, o	Legal checks to determine any existing legal agreements that cover the site or building.

Certainly! Here's a rephrased version:

• Conducting legal checks to identify any pre-existing legal agreements that pertain to the site or building in question.
Can you rewrite this, •	Description of the proposed usage of the land and/or building;

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Outlining the intended purpose or planned usage of the land or building.

Can you write this, •	Industry context, for example, current economic activity in the sectors relevant to the project and factors that may impact on the economic performance of the project when completed;

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Providing an overview of the industry context, including an analysis of the current economic activity within sectors relevant to the project, as well as identifying potential factors that could influence the economic performance of the project upon completion.
Can you rewrite this, •	Market sounding of industry participants to gauge interest in the project

Certainly! Here's a rephrased version:

• Engaging in market sounding activities with industry participants to assess their interest and gather feedback regarding the project.

Can you rewrite this, •	Market analysis taking account of the strengths and weaknesses and opportunities and threats that will influence project outcomes, populations size, demographics and growth, consumer demand, developer, and purchaser property investment preferences;

Certainly! Here's a revised version:

• Conducting a comprehensive market analysis that considers the strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats that will impact project outcomes, including factors such as population size, demographics, growth trends, consumer demand, as well as the preferences of developers and property investors.

Can you write this better, •	Market comparison looking at recent sales of comparable properties and/or projects, similar projects in the vicinity of the site, and developer and purchaser property investment preferences. Also examines the estimates cost of purchasing the property and or constructing the project, compared to similar recent projects;

Certainly! Here's an improved version:

• Conducting a thorough market comparison by examining recent sales of comparable properties and projects, evaluating similar projects in the vicinity of the site, and analyzing the preferences of developers and property investors. Additionally, the assessment includes an examination of the estimated cost of property acquisition and/or project construction, in comparison to similar recent projects.
